title: EU Announces 13 New Critical Raw Material Projects Outside the Bloc
content: The EU announced 13 new critical rawâ€material projects outside the bloc to reduce reliance on countries like China for essential minerals used in energy, defense and aerospace. Ten projects focus on electricâ€vehicle battery materials (lithium, cobalt, manganese, graphite), while two target rare earths in Malawi and South Africa and one in Greenland will extract graphite. Other initiatives include a UK tungsten project and a Serbian lithium mine capable of supplying 90 percent of Europeâ€™s needs. Together these ventures require an estimated â‚¬5.5 billion and bring the EUâ€™s total strategic projects to 60 under its 2023 Critical Raw Material Act.
author: Analysts Account
